toolCall.argumentsJson:
{"CommandLine":"python -c "
import socket
import subprocess
import time
import os

def check_and_launch_chrome():
    port = 9222
    with socket.socket(socket.AF_INET, socket.SOCK_STREAM) as s:
        is_open = s.connect_ex(('127.0.0.1', port)) == 0
    
    if not is_open:
        print('Chrome remote debugging is not running. Launching Chrome...')
        chrome_path = r'C:\Program Files\Google\Chrome\Application\chrome.exe'
        if not os.path.exists(chrome_path):
            chrome_path = r'C:\Program Files (x86)\Google\Chrome\Application\chrome.exe'
        
        user_data_dir = r'C:\selenium\AutomationProfile'
        os.makedirs(user_data_dir, exist_ok=True)
        
        # Use subprocess.Popen to launch Chrome detached
        subprocess.Popen([chrome_path, f'--remote-debugging-port={port}', f'--user-data-dir={user_data_dir}'])
        time.sleep(3)
        print('Chrome launched.')
    else:
        print('Chrome remote debugging is already running.')
        
check_and_launch_chrome()
"","Cwd":"C:\Users\smile\blog_auto","WaitMsBeforeAsync":2000,"toolAction":"Testing the logic to launch Chrome automatically if it's not running in debug mode","toolSummary":"Test Chrome auto-launch logic"}
